TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF OLD DITCH RD CAUSING NUMEROUS SNAPPED SOFTWOOD PINES. AS THE TORNADO PROGRESSED NORTHEAST, WIDTH INCREASED TO APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS WITH A CLEAR CONVERGENT PATTERN OF SNAPPED SOFTWOODS. AS THE TORNADO APPROACHED PIAVE CHURCH RD, IT APPEARS TO HAVE WEAKENED CAUSING SEVERAL UPROOTED HARDWOODS AND A FEW SNAPPED TO UPROOTED SOFTWOODS. A FEW HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE LIKELY BY THE OUTER CIRCULATION AND RFD. THE TORNADO SHORTLY LIFTED AFTER CROSSING PIAVE CHURCH RD BUT NOT BEFORE UPROOTING A COUPLE MORE HARDWOODS AND REMOVING A TIN ROOF FROM A MOBILE HOME PORCH.
DAMAGE TO TREES AND A COUPLE OF HOUSES ON PIAVE CHURCH ROAD BETWEEN UNION ROAD AND HIGHWAY 63. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

TREES FELL ONTO 2 HOMES AND 1 MOBILE HOME NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 17 AND BARLOW RD. NUMEROUS OTHER TREES WERE UPROOTED. A TORNADO PATH COULD NOT BE IDENTIFIED AND THIS DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A HEAVILY WOODED AREA WEST OF LASITER RD (ALSO WEST OF HIGHWAY 17.) THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS PINE TREES TREES ALONG ITS PATH. THE START AND END POINTS ARE ESTIMATED DUE TO LACK OF ACCESS. THERE WAS ALSO A MUCH LARGER AREA OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OBSERVED IN AND AROUND CULLOMBURG WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60-80 MPH, WHICH DOWNED NUMEROUS TREES AND HEAVILY DAMAGED A COUPLE OF SHEDS.
SEVERAL DOWNED TREES REPORTED AROUND CULLOMBURG AND BLADON SPRINGS.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY THREE QUARTERS OF A MILE WEST OF WEST BEND ROAD AND MOVED NORTHWEST ACROSS WEST BEND ROAD BEFORE LIFTING. NUMEROUS PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. THE START POINT OF THE PATH IS ESTIMATED. THERE WAS ALSO A MUCH LARGER AREA OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OBSERVED AROUND THE AREA, WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60-80 MPH, WHICH RESULTED IN TREE DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON HWY 69 BETWEEN COFFEEVILLE AND SALIPTA.
MOBILE HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE.

THE TORNADO ENTERED WILCOX COUNTY FROM MARENGO COUNTY JUST SOUTHWEST OF COUNTY ROAD 32 AND LIFTED NEAR COUNTY ROAD 30. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 32 WHERE NUMEROUS PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED. A MOBILE HOME WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED BY A LARGE PINE TREE. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH WITH THE TORNADO WEAKENING AS IT APPROACHED COUNTY ROAD 30 JUST EAST OF LAMISON.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE LONG TRACK TORNADO THAT STARTED IN SOUTHEASTERN WALTHALL COUNTY. THE TORNADO CROSSED INTO PERRY COUNTY FROM FORREST COUNTY ALONG THE LEAF RIVER BOTTOM, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES. IT CONTINUED TO TRACK JUST SOUTH OF OLD RIVER ROAD BEFORE DISSIPATING ALONG OLD RIVER ROAD 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF NEW AUGUSTA.

*** 1 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST TO THE NORTHWEST OF COUNTY ROAD 70 AND TRAVELED SOUTHEAST FOR ROUGHLY 1.25 MILES, PRODUCING EF-2 DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS STRUCTURES AND TREES ALONG THE WAY.
*** 1 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST TO THE NORTHWEST OF COUNTY ROAD 70 AND TRAVELED SOUTHEAST FOR ROUGHLY 1.25 MILES, PRODUCING EF-2 DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS STRUCTURES AND TREES ALONG THE WAY.
AWOS STATION K79J AT THE ANDALUSIA OPP AIRPORT MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 60 MPH.

THE EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF NORTH GRAND BAY WILMER ROAD AND CONTINUED EAST-SOUTHEAST TO ELIZA JORDAN ROAD SOUTH, THEN TURNED TOWARD THE SOUTHEAST CROSSING OVER AIRPORT BOULEVARD. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED AFTER THE TORNADO CROSSED AIRPORT BLVD. IN THE WAKEFIELD AND DOMINION SUBDIVISIONS WHERE MANY HOMES SUFFERED DAMAGE TO ROOFS, SIDING, GUTTERS, CHIMNEYS, AND PORCHES. NUMEROUS FENCES WERE BROUGHT DOWN AS WELL. THE TORNADO CONTINUED SOUTHEAST BEFORE FINALLY LIFTING JUST SOUTH OF COTTAGE HILL ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE ENTIRE PATH.
TREES DOWN IN OPP. METAL ROOF PEELED OFF RESIDENCE.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF THE ALABAMA/MISSISSIPPI STATE LINE AND TRACKED EAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 1.3 MILES, PRODUCING EF-0 AND EF-1 DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING JUST EAST OF TOM GASTON ROAD SOUTH. THE TORNADO TIPPED OVER AN IRRIGATION WATERING SYSTEM, DESTROYED 3 SILOS, 2 BARNS, TIPPED OVER 3 SEMI TRAILERS, AND CAUSED TREE DAMAGE.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF HITT ROAD IN THE SMITHFIELD SUBDIVISION AND TRACKED SOUTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 3.1 MILES BEFORE LIFTING JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HILLCREST RD AND COTTAGE HILL RD. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPORADIC EF-0 AND EF-1 DAMAGE ALONG THE WAY, PRIMARILY TO TREES AND FENCES. SOME HOMES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING LIMBS/TREES.

TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. TREE DAMAGED A BOAT TRAILER. LIMBS AND LEAF DEBRIS NOTED ALL ALONG MCFARLAND RD/SNOW RD AREA. SIMILAR DAMAGE IN THE SPRING GROVE NEIGHBORHOOD. REPORTS VIA HAM RADIO OPERATORS AND PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. FENCE BLOWN DOWN.
PINTO ISLAND (USS ALABAMA) MEASURED A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 43 MPH.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND THE DAMAGE IN THE SPANISH FORT AREA ALONG AND JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 31 WAS FROM STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70-80 MPH. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED IN THE AREA, INCLUDING ONE LARGE TREE ON AN APARTMENT COMPLEX BUILDING ON STAGECOACH RD. FENCE DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED ALONG WITH SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A FEW BUILDINGS. A CONVERGENT TORNADO PATH COULD NOT BE IDENTIFIED.
GAZEBO DESTROYED. TREES DOWN IN THE IRVINGTON AREA. REPORTS AND PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN IRVINGTON.
MESONET STATION ON THE DOG RIVER MEASURED A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 43 MPH.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN. TREE ON RESIDENCE.
USA CHILI MESONET SITE IN LOXLEY MEASURED WINDS OF 74 MPH.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE IN IN THE POINT CLEAR/FAIRHOPE AREA. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED IN THE BATTLES RD/OLD BATTLES ROAD AREAS WITH SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE NOTED FURTHER EAST ALONG COUNTY ROAD 32, EAST OF US HIGHWAY 98. WINDS ARE ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN 70-80 MPH. A CONVERGENT TORNADO PATH COULD NOT BE IDENTIFIED.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS HUXFORD. TRAMPOLINE TOSSED. PICTURES AND REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
REPORT OF A CAMPER OVERTURNED. TREES DOWN. PICTURES AND VIDEOS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

*** 1 INJ *** THE EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF COWPEN CREEK ROAD AND CONTINUED EAST-SOUTHEAST ACROSS THE ROAD. NUMEROUS LARGE PINES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES SUFFERED DAMAGE TO ROOFS, SIDING, AND SKIRTING. AN RV CAMPER WAS PICKED UP BY THE TORNADO AND DESTROYED. ONE INDIVIDUAL SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST WEST OF BURGETT LANE. STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE CONTINUED FARTHER EAST-SOUTHEAST ACROSS BURGETT LANE AND COUNTY ROAD 87.
MEDIA REPORT OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN THE MOLINO COMMUNITY. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A CORRIDOR OF SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE ON THE NORTH-SIDE OF CANTONMENT, FROM WEST QUINTETTE ROAD EAST SOUTHEAST TOWARD THE ESCAMBIA RIVER (SOUTH OF MCKENZIE ROAD AND EAST OF HIGHWAY95A.) ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO SOME HOMES WITH SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE OBSERVED IN THE AREA. WINDS ARE ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN 80-85 MPH. THE SURVEY COULD NOT IDENTIFY A CONVERGENT TORNADO PATH.
SEVERAL HOMES LOST THEIR FENCES AND SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGES. ALSO, A REPORT OF LUMBER SPIKED INTO THE GROUND IN A YARD. REPORTS AND PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND THAT MOST OF THE WIND DAMAGE THAT OCCURRED IN PACE, FL WAS THE RESULT OF 70-80 MPH STRAIGHT LINE WIND. THIS RESULTED IN NUMEROUS DOWN TREES ACROSS THE CITY AS WELL AS ROOF DAMAGE TO SOME RESIDENCES. THE SURVEY ALSO FOUND A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TRACK FROM EPHREM LN TO BRADLEY DR IN PACE, FL. IN THIS AREA, CONVERGENCE IN TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED, WHILE ACROSS THE REST OF PACE, THE PATH OF DAMAGE WAS FOUND TO BE ALL IN ONE DIRECTION.

WAYNE COUNTY EMA RELAYED A PICTURE OF A SNAPPED TREE THAN LANDED ON A HOME ON HARRY PIPPEN ROAD.

THE TORNADO FORMED NEAR EUCUTTA RD AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST, LIFTING JUST EAST OF SUGAR HILL RD. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES, SOME OF WHICH WERE UPROOTED AND SOME WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF-1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK OF 105 MPH.

TREES DOWN ON CHILTON ROAD.

TREES REPORTED DOWN IN THE COMMUNITY OF VREDENBURGH. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREES DOWN SOUTHWEST OF PERKINSTON (MAGNOLIA AREA). ROAD DEPT RELAYED TO EM EARLIER TODAY. NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE NOTED.

THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 63 ON INLAND BEACH ROAD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED MAINLY EAST AND CROSSED BEAVER DAM ROAD AND LIFTED JUST EAST OF JONES ROAD AND SOUTH OF NORTHSIDE CHURCH ROAD. THE TORNADO PRIMARILY RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO TREES, SNAPPING SOME SOFTWOOD TREES AND UPROOTING HARDWOOD TREES ALONG THE PATH. A FEW HOMES WERE DAMAGED BY THE DOWNED TREES AND A FEW HOMES EXPERIENCED SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. RATED EF-1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK OF 95 MPH.

A BRIEF AND NARROW EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF PASSEAU ROAD AND MOVED EAST ACROSS ROCKY CREEK ROAD. THE TORNADO PRODUCED HEAVY DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME AT THE INTERSECTION OF ROCKY CREEK ROAD AND DAVID WADE ROAD, REMOVING THE ROOF WHICH THEN RESULTED IN THE COLLAPSE OF THE WALLS ON THE BACK SIDE OF THE STRUCTURE. SEVERAL PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. ESTIMATED PEAK 95 MPH.

*** 1 INJ *** DAMAGE TO AT LEAST 9 STRUCTURES IN THE ROCKY CREEK AREA. A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. A TRAVEL TRAILER WAS FLIPPED, RESULTING IN A MINOR INJURY TO AN OCCUPANT. SEVERAL HOMES HAVE ROOF DAMAGE AND THERE IS DAMAGE TO A CHURCH. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
DAMAGE REPORTED TO HOMES, TREES, AND POWER LINES IN THE ROCKY CREEK AREA.

*** 1 INJ *** ANOTHER TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF U.S. HIGHWAY 98 AND TRAVELED EAST, PARALLELING JUST NORTH OF OLD MOBILE HIGHWAY AND GORDON ROAD, BEFORE LIFTING NEAR ODOM ROAD. THIS TORNADO RESULTED IN SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE, SNAPPING A LARGE AMOUNT OF PINE TREES. NUMEROUS LARGE OAK TREES WERE ALSO UPROOTED. THE DOWNED TREES CAUSED DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES. A FEW OTHER HOMES SUFFERED SHINGLE AND ROOF DAMAGE. THERE WAS ALSO ROOF DAMAGE TO A CHURCH AT THE SPLIT BETWEEN OLD MOBILE HIGHWAY AND GORDON ROAD. A TRAVEL TRAILER ON OLD MOBILE HIGHWAY WAS FLIPPED, RESULTING IN A MINOR INJURY TO ONE PERSON. RATED EF-1 WITH A PEAK OF 105 MPH.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST BETWEEN LOTT ROAD AND MALONE ROAD AND MOVED EAST ACROSS MALONE ROAD. THE TORNADO PRIMARILY RESULTED IN TREE DAMAGE. HOWEVER, ONE LARGE TREE RESULTED IN SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A HOME. ONE RESIDENCE ALSO HAD ITS GARAGE DOOR BLOWN IN AND A SHED WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED. RATED EF-1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK OF 90 MPH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG VICKIE LEIGH ROAD AND QUICKLY INTENSIFIED TO EF-1 STRENGTH. THERE WAS A PATH OF NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 90 MPH IN THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AND PRODUCED INTERMITTENT DAMAGE OVER THE NEXT 2.7 MILES AS IT MOVED THROUGH THE OCEAN CITY SECTION OF NORTH FORT WALTON BEACH. DAMAGE IN THESE LOCATIONS WERE IN THE EF-0 INTENSITY WITH TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO HOME SIDING AND ROOF SHINGLES.

SEVERAL TREES DOWN. TREE FELL AND DAMAGED A CARPORT AND HOUSE. PICTURES AND VIDEO VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

A TORNADO TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED JUST NORTH OF THE GEORGE AND JACKSON COUNTY LINE AND CONTINUED JUST OVER 1 MILE TOWARDS THE NORTHEAST. NUMEROUS PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED NEAR THE TRUNK. THE APPROXIMATE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF CCC ROAD AND TRAVELED JUST LESS THAN ONE MILE TO THE NORTHEAST ENDING AT THE INTERSECTION OF HILLTOP DRIVE AND MARSHALL SMITH ROAD. A FEW TREE TOPS WERE DAMAGED IN A PASTURE WITH A STRAND OF TREES UPROOTED ALONG A FENCE LINE. THERE WAS MINOR DAMAGE TO A METAL ROOF OF A HOUSE ALONG WITH METAL ROOFING TORN FROM A SHED. THE APPROXIMATE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.
TREES AND POWER POLES SNAPPED. POWER LINES DOWN. METAL WRAPPED AROUND A POWER POLE. PICTURES AND REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

A TORNADO BEGAN JUST SOUTH OF PARKER ROAD AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES, CROSSING NENA ROAD AND HWY. 613, BEFORE ENDING IN A PASTURE JUST SOUTH OF CORLEY ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG PARKER ROAD. IN ADDITION, A ROOF ON A HOME WAS DAMAGED AND SEVERAL METAL PANELS WERE TORN FROM AN OUTBUILDING. ALONG NENA ROAD, AN OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND A MOBILE HOME WAS DAMAGED. THERE WERE ALSO SEVERAL ADDITIONAL TREES SNAPPED. ANOTHER OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED BY A FALLEN OAK TREE ALONG HWY. 613, WITH ANOTHER LARGE OAK TREE UPROOTED AT THE END OF THE TORNADO PATH JUST SOUTH OF CORLEY ROAD.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES SNAPPED NEAR ZEIGLER BLVD AND CODY RD. ANOTHER TREE DOWN NEAR OVERLOOK RD. TRAFFIC SIGNALS DAMAGED. REPORTS AND PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

A VERY BRIEF, WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF ZEIGLER BLVD ON DURBIN COURT AND TRACKED NORTHEAST, LIFTING AS IT APPROACHED THE MOBILE FAIRGROUNDS. THE DAMAGE WITH THE TORNADO WAS VERY SPORADIC AND LIGHT, WITH MAINLY BRANCHES OF SOFTWOOD TREES DAMAGED. IT SHOULD BE NOTED THAT MOST OF THE NOTABLE DAMAGE WAS FROM 60 TO 70 MPH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS THAT CONTINUED FURTHER TO THE NORTH AND EAST TOWARD THE OVERLOOK DR AND MOFFETT ROAD AREAS, RESULTING IN SPORADIC TREE, POWER LINE, AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. THANKS TO MOBILE COUNTY EMA FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE.
TREES DOWN IN A FEW SPOTS ALONG OLD SHELL RD. REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
TREES DOWN ON BOTH WOLF RIDGE RD AND FEED MILL RD. REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED WITH LARGE TREES UPROOTED ALONG KELLY TRAIL.

THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHWEST OF KELLY TRAIL AND MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS THE ROAD. IN THIS AREA, SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO BOTH SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES WITH THE TRUNKS OF MANY TREES SNAPPED. A COUPLE OF HOMES SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE DUE TO THE FALLEN TREES. ANOTHER HOME ON THE WESTERN EDGE OF THE TORNADO PATH HAD ROOF DAMAGE TO THE NORTH SIDE OF THE STRUCTURE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND CROSSED CELESTE ROAD, WEAKENED AND LIFTED JUST EAST OF JOHN KELLY ROAD. THE TREE DAMAGE WAS MUCH MORE SPORADIC AND NOT AS SIGNIFICANT IN THE CELESTE ROAD AREA AS IT WAS FURTHER TO THE SOUTHWEST.
